sections.heading: Repository context to read
sections.body:
- `PRD_HealthTech.md` — product requirements, personas, epics/user stories, security spec, and NFRs.
- `BACKLOG.md` — milestones M0–M4, issues #1–#31, dependency graph, and recommended implementation order.
- The specific GitHub issue being worked (`kortiene/HealthTech`) and its acceptance criteria.
- The existing source tree as it actually exists now. This project began greenfield; do not assume an app, backend, test command, or stack exists until you verify it on disk.

sections.heading: Product summary
sections.body: `HealthTech` is a decentralized, local-first / zero-knowledge digital health platform for Côte d'Ivoire, serving patients and health professionals. The patient carries their medical record on their smartphone and grants ephemeral, controlled access to professionals via a dynamic QR code, without depending on permanent internet connectivity. The cloud stores only opaque encrypted blobs keyed by anonymous UUIDs and must never read or decrypt medical data.

sections.heading: Target component map
sections.body:
- Patient app: mobile-first, Android focus on entry-level devices; local account creation, master-key generation, record encryption, QR generation, zero-knowledge cloud backup, key recovery.
- Health-professional interface: web/PWA + mobile; QR scan, RAM-only decryption, note/prescription editing, end-of-session re-encryption + cloud sync + RAM wipe, offline queue.
- Zero-knowledge backend: minimal blob store (`PUT/GET /blob/{uuid}`), hosted on Ivorian soil; never sees plaintext or keys.
- Shared crypto core: AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption, master-key management, PBKDF2 key derivation/recovery.
- Infra/compliance: sovereign hosting, secrets management, CI/CD, ARTCI homologation artifacts.

sections.heading: Security and privacy constraints
sections.body:
- Local-first / zero-knowledge is non-negotiable: the medical record is encrypted client-side with AES-256-GCM before any network transit.
- Never weaken cryptography; never log or persist plaintext medical data, encryption keys, or PII.
- Access is ephemeral and patient-controlled: QR codes expire around 120 seconds; professionals decrypt in RAM only; sessions are wiped on end/inactivity.
- Data residency: data must stay hosted on Ivorian soil to satisfy ARTCI / loi n°2013-450.
- Degraded-network resilience: tolerate power/network cuts; support offline queueing; plaintext record stays <= 500 KB; heavy medical images are never stored on the patient device, only ephemeral URLs.
- The master key is generated on-device and sealed in the hardware keystore; it is never exported in clear.

sections.heading: Current status and assumptions
sections.body: HealthTech started as a greenfield project. Verify the current state from the repository before acting. If the stack or test command is still unset, do not assume Cargo/Rust, npm, Flutter, backend language, object storage, or CI conventions. Work from the PRD, BACKLOG, and the specific issue.

sections.heading: Working rules
sections.body:
- Identify the owning component before editing.
- Respect BACKLOG dependency order; crypto core (#10) and zero-knowledge backend (#9) gate much downstream work; the consultation loop (#16→#19) is core demonstrable value.
- Preserve the zero-knowledge boundary and local-first/offline-resilient design in every change.
- Keep changes focused, idiomatic, testable, and production-minded.
- Update relevant docs, ADRs, schemas, threat-model, or compliance artifacts when behavior changes.
- The orchestrator owns all git/gh operations; do not run git or gh yourself inside ADW phases.

sections.heading: Verification guidance
sections.body: Run the project's configured test gate when available (`ADW_TEST_CMD`) plus any format/lint/build checks the repo defines. If no test command is configured, state that clearly and recommend the exact command(s) to run once the stack lands; do not invent a toolchain.

metaprompt.instructions: Refine the deterministic ADW phase prompt into a HealthTech-specific runtime prompt. Preserve YAML frontmatter exactly. Preserve all runtime argument tokens exactly (`$1`, `$2`, `$ARGUMENTS`, `$@`, `${@:2}`, etc.). Preserve ADW phase boundaries, required output/artifact instructions, and orchestrator-owned git/gh rules. Improve clarity and phase-specific guidance using only the profile data; do not invent implemented features, stack choices, test commands, or compliance claims.

phaseGuidance.plan: Emphasize PRD/BACKLOG reading, dependency order, security/privacy constraints, implementation roadmap, test strategy, and acceptance criteria. Do not implement.
phaseGuidance.implement: Emphasize scoped production-grade implementation, preserving zero-knowledge and local-first constraints, and verifying against the configured gate.
phaseGuidance.tests: Emphasize deterministic non-e2e tests, security/privacy regressions, and avoiding live infrastructure/hardware unless the repo already provides a harness.
phaseGuidance.e2e_tests: Emphasize cross-boundary flows such as QR access, session lifecycle, offline/sync, crypto boundaries, and patient/professional interactions.
phaseGuidance.review_phase: Emphasize blocker-grade review of security/privacy, zero-knowledge boundaries, acceptance criteria, and test/documentation adequacy.
phaseGuidance.document: Emphasize updating broader project docs, ADRs, compliance/threat-model artifacts, and developer guidance only when warranted.
